name: Kushli
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour, salt, and baking soda together.
2. Rub in 2 tablespoons butter/oil until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
3. Prepare a stiff dough by gradually adding water (about 1/2 cup) to the dry ingredients; mix until combined. Set aside.
4. In a skillet, heat remaining butter/oil over medium heat.
5. Add coconut, almonds, and cream of wheat to the skillet. Cook until the mixture is golden brown.
6. Remove seeds from the cardamom pods and add them along with sugar and raisins to the skillet. Cook for 5-7 minutes or until the sugar has completely dissolved.
7. Divide dough into 12 equal portions. Roll each portion into a 4-inch round, leaving a 1/4 inch border around the edge.
8. Place about half of the filling mixture on one side of the round, stopping just short of the edge. Fold the other half over to enclose the filling completely and seal the edges.
9. Heat oil for deep frying in a large skillet or pot until it reaches about 350°F (175°C).
10. Fry the pastries until golden brown, turning occasionally for even cooking.
